title: Valid Tic-Tac-Toe State
hints: Count the total number of X's and O's on the board. What relationship must always hold between these two counts in a valid game?
Check if either player has formed a winning line. If both have winning lines, the state is invalid. If only one has, does the turn count match the winner?
If X wins, X must have just moved, so count(X) equals count(O)+1. If O wins, count(X) must equal count(O). Use these rules to validate the state.
